claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. 

I have one child - his name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I have one child to assess - Alex.

Before I check the specific eligibility rules, I need to gather some details about Alex and your circumstances.

**First, can you tell me how old Alex is?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

**Next, I need to ask: Does Alex currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to understand your residency status to assess eligibility.

**Are you a UK resident, and what is your current immigration status? For example, are you a British citizen, settled status holder, or do you have another type of visa or status?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK resident. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming you're a UK resident in Manchester.

I need a bit more detail about your immigration status to complete the eligibility assessment.

**What is your specific immigration status? For example, are you a British citizen, do you have settled status, or another type of visa?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that clarification.

I just need one more piece of information to complete my assessment.

**Are you currently working in the UK, or are you receiving any state benefits? Also, do you have any income, and if so, roughly what is your annual income?** (This helps me understand if there are any income-related factors that might affect your eligibility.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do work, yes. Why do you need to know about my income? I thought Child Benefit was available to everyone?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's a great question! You're right that Child Benefit is quite broadly available. I apologize for any confusion - let me clarify.

The main eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are actually based on:
- Your residency and immigration status (which you've confirmed ✓)
- The child's age and circumstances (which we've covered for Alex ✓)
- Who the child lives with

Income doesn't typically affect basic Child Benefit eligibility itself. However, there is a separate tax charge called the "High Income Child Benefit Charge" that applies if your income exceeds a certain threshold - but that's a tax matter rather than an eligibility matter.

So you're absolutely right - if you meet the basic criteria, you can receive Child Benefit!

Let me now check the full eligibility rules to give you a definitive answer about Alex. Let me do that now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide you with my assessment.

**You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

✓ You are a British citizen and UK resident (living in Manchester)
✓ Alex is 9 years old (under 16, so within the eligible age range)
✓ Alex lives with you (you are responsible for him and he lives in your household)

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Since all of these conditions are met, Alex qualifies for Child Benefit.

The only thing to be aware of is the High Income Child Benefit tax charge - if your income exceeds £60,000 per year, you may have to pay a tax charge on the Child Benefit received. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to receive it; it's just a tax matter.

You should be able to apply for Child Benefit for Alex. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great news, thank you so much for your help! I really appreciate you explaining everything clearly. I'll go ahead and apply for it now.
